slides.kicker: // sample template · developer tools & platform engineering
slides.lead: Demo (sample) presentation template. All text is placeholder (dummy) and only shows off the styles.
slides.notes: Title slide. Put the event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Wrap a word in em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared developer experience.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions in between.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Build queues, flaky pipelines and the wait for a working environment.
slides.notes: Divider slide between parts. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Internal tools win the same way external ones do: real users, fast feedback and a roadmap. Treat developers as customers.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter opener.

items: median CI run after caching and test sharding
more deploys per week with self-service pipelines
fewer onboarding tickets after one golden path
bullets: Measure flow, not activity: what reaches production is what counts.
Start where the pain is loudest and the data already exists.
A small paved road beats a big platform nobody adopts.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The default option is the safe and fast one.
bullets: A new service with CI, logging and alerts wired in.
One blessed stack so teams stop reinventing setup.
Policy and security checks run before merge.
One command from commit to a running preview.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text auto-scales.

slides.lead: Same request, two paths and two very different lead times.
cards: Every change waits on the platform team. Slow and a bottleneck.
Teams clone old repos and drift apart over time.
A portal and templates handle the common cases instantly.
Self-service for the common path, experts for the edge cases.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing options.

slides.lead: Six common building blocks of an internal platform.
cards: One place to find every service and its owner.
Reusable pipelines so teams skip the boilerplate.
Reproducible, ready in minutes, not days.
Logs, traces and dashboards on by default.
Guardrails baked into the path, not bolted on.
Runbooks and guides that live next to the code.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Teams try the platform once, hit friction and drift back.
Adoption grows team by team as trust builds.
Each team that joins makes the next one faster to onboard.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: You do not adopt a platform because it exists. You adopt it because the paved road is the fastest way to ship.
slides.by: Sample quote to show off the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: The paved road must be easier than the workaround.
Real users, feedback loops and a public roadmap.
The platform team builds on the platform too.
slides.notes: A plain bulleted slide with a strong lead word. Text auto-scales to fill the area.

slides.lead: A mandate without a great experience just breeds shadow tooling.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the portal and scaffold a running service live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
